_readme: Authoritative manual coordinate overrides for hinterland communities. Keyed by region + lower(name) (must match a communities row). Each entry either pins a coordinate (applied as geocode_source='manual', geocode_confidence='high', winning over any geocoded value) or excludes a pin ("exclude": true -> latitude/longitude forced NULL, an honest un-geocoded state). Only add coordinates confirmed from an authoritative source (cite it in "source"). Applied idempotently by scripts/hinterland/apply-manual-coords.js. Extensible: add entries here and re-run the applier. Seeded 2026-07-01 from a two-stage research+cross-verify pass (each coordinate agreed by >=2 independent gazetteers within ~5km).

entries.name: Santa Mission
entries.source: Wikipedia + Wikidata + GeoNames (agree ~250m); OSM Nominatim verify
entries.note: Arawak/Lokono village on Kamuni Creek. Upgraded from low; confirmed the existing pin was correct.

entries.name: Mabaruma Central
entries.note: Barima-Waini regional capital; central sub-area pinned at Mabaruma town.

entries.name: Matthew's Ridge
entries.note: Confirms the OSM geocode; recorded as manual for provenance.

entries.name: Paruima
entries.note: Akawaio village near the Venezuela border (Kamarang area).

entries.name: 8 mile
entries.note: Existing pin 8.31,-59.82 was ~130 km off (Mabaruma/Kamwatta sub-district). The real hamlet is a mile-marker settlement on the Seven-Mile Branch Road tied to Waramuri (Moruca/Santa Rosa, ~7.6,-58.87), but no gazetteer publishes a point coordinate, so it stays honestly un-geocoded.

entries.name: Mora
entries.source: OSM: the resolved 'Mora' (6.836,-58.449) is a Region-3 Essequibo Islands hamlet, not Region 1
entries.note: Existing pin 6.84,-58.45 is a different Mora (Region 3). The Region-1 Mora is only a sub-settlement of Santa Rosa (Moruca) with no published coordinate; left un-geocoded rather than mis-pinned.
